pinned_source_hash_note: SHA-256 of the three source modules the generator imports, as they exist at pinned_source_commit. The generator fails closed (no fixture writes) unless the transformers install it actually imports hashes to these exact values -- computed once by fetching each file's raw content at the pinned commit and hashing it; regenerate by running under `uv run --with "transformers @ git+https://github.com/huggingface/transformers@ab1771c9e42891d893189978a8009426d70b4688"`, which installs exactly this commit.
expected_rope_parameters_note: Gemma4TextConfig(**e2b_config.json['text_config']).rope_parameters, computed by the pinned reference config class from the committed E2B config fixture. The generator asserts the live reference-derived value equals this before deriving the theta/partial-factor scalars used by the dual_rope golden's synthetic (reduced-head_dim) tensors, and before writing any fixture.
mutation_separation_floor_note: Predeclared per-op minimum required max-abs-diff for a mutation (wrong-implementation) negative test to pass, independent of any observed lattice result: tolerance_max_abs_diff * mutation_separation_floor_multiplier. A negative test asserting only `diff > tolerance` is decorative near the boundary; `diff >= mutation_separation_floor` demands the mutation diverge by a full order of magnitude past the golden's own noise floor.
fixture_format: Each op has a <op>.json sidecar (shapes/scalars/tensor refs) plus one <op>_<field>.bin per tensor field (raw little-endian float32, shape/sha256 recorded in the sidecar's ref).

rms_norm.tolerance_justification: Elementwise reduction (square, mean, +eps, pow(-0.5), multiply) over a width-64 f32 accumulator, single reduction pass, no matmul reassociation. For an n=64 naive summation of O(1)-scale inputs, worst-case f32 round-off accumulates roughly linearly in n and per-element unit roundoff (~1.19e-7), placing the bound an order of magnitude below 1e-6. The lattice kernel computes sqrt(mean+eps) then reciprocal rather than the reference's single pow(x,-0.5) call -- two rounding steps instead of one -- which the declared tolerance also covers; this is bounded f32 parity, not bit-for-bit equivalence (see gemma4_ops.rs's RMSNorm doc comment).

rms_norm_wide.tolerance_justification: Same op as rms_norm, at the real E2B hidden_size=1536 reduction width (24x the other RMSNorm golden's width=64). Naive-summation round-off accumulates roughly linearly in reduction width; scaling the width=64 structural bound by that ratio, and adding headroom for reduction-order differences between torch's summation and the lattice SIMD partial-sum tree (four-wide NEON accumulators merged pairwise, a different association order than a linear scan), yields a conservative ceiling that stays several orders of magnitude below the O(1)-scale divergence a wrong-formula or reduction-order bug would produce.

geglu_mlp.tolerance_justification: Two hidden x intermediate matmuls (gate_proj, up_proj, 64x96 reduction) and one intermediate x hidden matmul (down_proj, 96x64 reduction), chained through one tanh-GELU nonlinearity. The reference uses torch's BLAS reduction order; the lattice kernel under test uses Accelerate/AMX or a scalar/SIMD fallback, so reduction order (and therefore rounding) can differ from torch's at each of the three matmuls, per this repo's f32-reassociation-reorder bug class. The declared tolerance accommodates that reordering compounding across a 3-matmul chain while remaining orders of magnitude below a wrong-formula, wrong-activation, or wrong-branch-order bug.

scaled_embedding.tolerance_justification: A table lookup (exact copy, no arithmetic) followed by one scalar multiply by a precomputed constant (embed_scale = sqrt(hidden_size)) -- no reduction, no accumulation, no reassociation risk; a single f32 multiply is exact to rounding for this magnitude range, so the declared tolerance is dominated by that one rounding step.

qk_norm_v_unscaled.tolerance_justification: Same elementwise-reduction shape and formula as rms_norm, applied per-head over head_dim in {16, 32} -- narrower than rms_norm's width-64 reduction, so its structural round-off bound is at least as tight as that op's.

logit_softcap.tolerance_justification: Two scalar multiplies and one tanh, elementwise, no reduction. Rust's f32::tanh and PyTorch's libm tanh are both correctly-rounded-to-a-few-ULPs implementations of the same mathematical function but not the same routine, so outputs can differ by a small constant number of ULPs across the operating range (including the saturation region where |logits/cap| is large); the tolerance is set to a small multiple of f32 ULP magnitude at cap=30 scale, several orders of magnitude below the divergence a disabled or misordered softcap would produce.

dual_rope.tolerance_justification: cos/sin evaluated once per (position, frequency) pair via a single sin/cos call each, then combined via two multiplies and one add per element (rotate_half) -- elementwise, no reduction, matching the reference's own float32 RoPE math term-for-term. The only source of divergence is per-call transcendental (sin/cos) ULP differences between Rust's libm and PyTorch's, several orders of magnitude below the divergence a pairing-convention or theta bug would produce.
